prompt:
Design-system request. Every squad has its own copy-pasted className mangling in the component library and they all disagree about dedupe, so I got sign-off to write classkit, our own tiny composer — we are deliberately NOT adding a runtime dependency for this, the lib itself has to be pure ESM with an empty dependencies block. The test stack is a different story: the suite is written for vitest, which is pinned as a devDependency in package.json with the lockfile committed. Nothing is installed on this box yet — the runner can't even start until you bring the env up from package-lock.json with npm ci (never bare `npm install` on shared machines), because `bash run_verify.sh` execs `npx --no-install vitest run` straight out of the project's node_modules.

Build src/classkit.mjs with two exports:

cx(...inputs) -> string. Accepts any mix of: strings (may hold several space-separated tokens), arrays (recursed, any depth), and plain objects (keys — themselves possibly multi-token — whose values are truthy contribute). Everything else — numbers, booleans, null, undefined, empty strings — contributes nothing. Tokens come out in first-appearance order, deduplicated (first one wins), joined by single spaces; no leading/trailing whitespace, and no input at all gives "".

variants(config) -> a render function for component recipes. config carries: base (class string, optional), variants (object: axis -> {value: class string}), defaults (axis -> value, optional), compound (optional array of {when: {axis: value, ...}, use: classes} applied in order when EVERY entry of `when` matches the effective selection — defaults count). The returned function is render(opts = {}, ...extras): effective selection = defaults overridden by opts, where an explicitly-undefined opt falls back like it wasn't given; an axis with no default and no opt just contributes nothing. Output order: base, then each chosen axis's classes in the order the axes are declared in config.variants, then matching compound classes, then the extras (which take anything cx takes) — the whole thing flowing through cx so dedupe is global. Guard rails: an opts key that isn't a declared axis throws Error("unknown variant: <axis>") and a value that isn't declared for its axis throws Error("unknown <axis>: <value>"), exact wording.

The contract is test/classkit.test.mjs (protected, as are the manifests). Done = `bash run_verify.sh` green.
